commit.message:
feat(platform): native helper binary — Retina crop fix, permission check, recording lifecycle hardening (#67)

* fix(platform): use CGDisplayBounds for display index — no Automation permission

Replace osascript + AppKit NSScreen approach in getWindowDisplayIndex() with
swift -e + CoreGraphics CGDisplayBounds. The previous implementation used
'use framework "AppKit"' in AppleScript, which routes through Apple Events
and triggers macOS's Automation permission dialog ('Visual Studio Code would
like to access data from other apps') before any recording starts.

CGDisplayBounds is a direct CoreGraphics framework call — no Apple Events,
no Automation permission required. The window's x/y coordinates (from the
existing getWindowBounds() call) are inlined into the Swift snippet, avoiding
the need to pass CommandLine.arguments through swift -e.

Falls back to display index 0 on any error or on systems without swift -e
(pre-macOS 12 / pre-Xcode 14 command line tools).

Removes: writeFile, unlink (fs/promises), tmpdir (os), join (path) imports
Adds: execFile (child_process)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: precompile swift display-index binary to eliminate swift -e hang

Replace the per-call `swift -e` invocation in getWindowDisplayIndex() with a
precompiled native binary at ~/.cache/gecho/display-index.

Problem: `swift -e` JIT-compiles the inline Swift snippet on every call, taking
10-30 s on first invocation (the 5 s timeout meant it always timed out and
returned 0, but still blocked start() for 5 seconds). This created a race where
stop() could be called while ffmpegProcess was still null, producing a confusing
'Recording failed — no output was written' error.

Fix (platform.ts):
- Write DISPLAY_INDEX_SOURCE to ~/.cache/gecho/display-index.swift on first use
  and compile it to ~/.cache/gecho/display-index with swiftc -O.
- Cache the compilation as a module-level Promise (compiledBinaryPath) so
  concurrent callers share the same in-flight compilation.
- The on-disk binary is reused across VS Code sessions — sub-millisecond after
  the one-time compile. Falls back to 0 on any error.
- Passes wx/wy as CLI args to the binary, so display index is always computed
  from the current window position (not stale data).

Fix (capture.ts):
- Add _startPromise and _stopRequested fields to ScreenCapture.
- Refactor start() to delegate to _doStart() and track the in-flight Promise.
- _doStart() checks _stopRequested after getWindowDisplayIndex() returns and
  aborts without spawning ffmpeg if stop() was called during the slow wait.
- stop() sets _stopRequested and awaits the pending _startPromise before
  checking ffmpegProcess, eliminating the null-process race condition.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(capture): stop() no longer hangs when ffmpeg ignores SIGINT

- Accept exit codes 0, 254, 255, 130 and signal-killed exits (signal != null)
  so all interrupt/kill variants resolve instead of rejecting
- Escalate: SIGTERM after 3s, SIGKILL after 5s if SIGINT is not honoured
- Clear escalation timers on close so they do not fire after process exits

Fixes: clicking stop recording appearing to do nothing on macOS

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(capture): use stdin q to stop ffmpeg, matching Chronicler pattern

SIGINT does not reliably stop AVFoundation ffmpeg processes on macOS
when sent programmatically (not from a TTY). Instead, write q to
ffmpeg stdin — the canonical programmatic stop mechanism.

Changes:
- spawn with explicit stdio: pipe so proc.stdin is always writable
- stop() writes q to stdin; SIGTERM/SIGKILL remain as fallback at 3s/5s
- Resolve unconditionally on close when WE initiated stop (exit code
  is expected to be non-zero on interrupt, per Chronicler behaviour)
- dispose() updated to use same q + SIGTERM pattern

This eliminates the 5s hang and the missing mp4 file (SIGKILL was
preventing ffmpeg from flushing the output before this fix).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(capture): fragmented mp4, stdin EOF stop, saving-gif state

Three issues addressed:

1. UI feedback: add saving-gif state shown immediately when the user clicks
   stop, before ffmpeg finalises the file. Status bar shows a spinner so
   the 1-2s wait does not feel like a hang.

2. Missing mp4: ffmpeg buffers an entire GOP before writing frames to disk.
   With the default keyframe interval (~250 frames) and ultrafast preset,
   the file could be empty if recording is stopped quickly. Fix: add
   -g <fps> (one keyframe per second) and -movflags +frag_keyframe+empty_moov
   so each fragment is flushed to disk progressively. The output file exists
   and contains valid data from the first second of recording onwards.

3. More reliable stop: change proc.stdin.write("q") to proc.stdin.end("q").
   This sends the ffmpeg quit keystroke AND closes stdin (sends EOF), giving
   ffmpeg two signals to exit gracefully. SIGTERM/SIGKILL escalation at 3s/5s
   remain as a safety net.

4. File verification: stop() now stats the output file after the process
   closes and rejects with a clear message if the file is missing or empty,
   rather than propagating a cryptic ffmpeg "No such file" error.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: make ffmpeg stop timeout configurable via gecho.recording.stopTimeoutMs

Add a new VS Code setting gecho.recording.stopTimeoutMs (default 15 s,
range 5–60 s) that controls how long to wait for ffmpeg to exit
gracefully before sending SIGKILL.

- SIGTERM fires at 50 % of the timeout (halfway point)
- SIGKILL fires at the full timeout value
- All stop() call sites in extension.ts now pass the live config value
- capture.ts stop() accepts stopTimeoutMs parameter (default 15_000)
- Also fixes waitForReady() to watch stderr for 'Output #0' so
  setState('recording-gif') is only set once ffmpeg has actually opened
  the output file, not just after the first banner line

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: restore setState('recording-gif') after waitForReady

Accidentally dropped in the stopTimeoutMs config refactor.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: reject waitForReady on timeout + starting-gif spinner state

Previously waitForReady silently resolved after 8 s even if ffmpeg had
never opened the output file, leaving the extension in 'recording-gif'
state while nothing was actually being recorded. SIGKILL then fired
during stop() before any bytes were written.

Changes:
- waitForReady now REJECTS on timeout when 'Output #0' was never seen,
  surfacing a clear error instead of silently lying about recording state
- Default startup timeout raised from 8 s → 20 s to give AVFoundation
  more headroom on slow machines
- New gecho.recording.startupTimeoutMs setting (5–60 s, default 20 s)
  lets users tune this for their hardware
- New 'starting-gif' RecordingState shows a spinner in the status bar
  while ffmpeg is initialising ('Starting...')
- setState('starting-gif') fires immediately when the user clicks start,
  so the UI is never frozen on 'idle'

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(jit): lazy Function.prototype creation in LdaNamedProperty stubs

When fn_props_get(ba, 'prototype') returns Undefined on first access,
create the prototype object lazily instead of returning JIT_DEOPT.
This eliminates spurious deopts for all benchmarks using constructors
(prototype_chain, object_creation, etc.).

Also return JIT_UNDEFINED for other undefined function properties
instead of deopting, preventing unnecessary interpreter fallback.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

- zgard init: Bootstrap package infrastructure (pkgs dir, env file, init script)

- Example DAG: sdkman → jdk → maven dependency chain

fix(encoders): move train_data to CPU in data-driven strategies

The whitened, class_contrast, and local_pca encoder strategies
receive train_data which may be on GPU (e.g. from ConvNEFPipeline
conv features). The torch.Generator for random sampling is always
CPU-bound, causing device mismatches during matrix operations.

Add .cpu() at the entry point of each strategy. The results are
moved to CPU by NEFLayer.__init__ anyway, so this is consistent.

* ci(sonarqube): pin actions to full commit SHAs

SonarQube flags `actions/checkout@v4` and
`SonarSource/sonarqube-scan-action@v6` because tag references are
mutable — a maintainer (or attacker with repo write access) can move
the tag to point at a different commit. Pinning to a full commit SHA
freezes the resolved code, with the version retained as a comment for
human readability and easy upgrades.

- actions/checkout v4 → 34e114876b0b11c390a56381ad16ebd13914f8d5
- SonarSource/sonarqube-scan-action v6 → fd88b7d7ccbaefd23d8f36f73b59db7a3d246602

commit.message:
feat(rules): add 6 new formatting and lint rules (#24)

## Summary

Implements 6 of the 8 rules identified in issue #22 from a review of a
real-world DuckDB query.

## Formatting rules

### `LEFT OUTER JOIN` → `LEFT JOIN`
Drops the redundant `OUTER` keyword from `LEFT` and `RIGHT` joins,
mirroring the existing bare `JOIN` → `INNER JOIN` normalization. `FULL
OUTER JOIN` is preserved.

### `GROUP BY` — one expression per line
Each `GROUP BY` expression now appears on its own line using the
leading-comma style, parallel to the existing `AND`/`OR` rule. `GROUP BY
ALL` stays inline.

## Lint rules (default: `warn`, configurable via `jarify.toml`)

| Rule | Description |
|------|-------------|
| `prefer-group-by-all` | Flag explicit `GROUP BY col1, col2` when all
non-aggregated SELECT columns are listed; suggest `GROUP BY ALL` |
| `prefer-using-over-on` | Flag `ON a.col = b.col` equi-joins where both
sides share a column name; suggest `USING (col)` |
| `consistent-empty-array` | Flag `'[]'::type[]` string-cast empty
arrays; prefer native `[]` literal (handles both direct cast and
COALESCE fallback patterns) |
| `no-select-star-in-cte` | Flag `SELECT *` inside CTE body definitions
(stricter sibling of `no-select-star`) |

## Other changes
- All rules wired into `JarifyConfig` with severity defaults
- Style guide updated with bad/good examples for every new rule
- `real_world` fixture snapshot updated to reflect `LEFT OUTER JOIN`
normalization
- 23 new tests (82 total, all passing)

## Deferred
Rules for multi-line struct constructor alignment and
`DISTINCT`-inside-aggregates formatting require deeper sqlglot AST
investigation and are tracked in a follow-up issue.

Closes #22

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

lando info returns healthy: "unknown" (truthy string in PHP) for
stopped containers, causing isRunning() to incorrectly report true.

Switch primary check to `lando list --format=json` which provides a
reliable boolean `running` field per container queried from Docker.

healthy string comparison (only accept true or "healthy", not any
truthy value).

Pass site name to isRunning() from checkRealStatus() so the lando list
lookup is exact-matched rather than basename-derived.

Add a refresh button next to the status dot so users can re-check the
real container state on demand.

Add Cancer Cell Fraction and Clonality to Plots Tab (#5492)

The Plots tab had no support for ASCN-derived mutation annotations. This adds **Cancer Cell Fraction** (numeric, 0–1) and **Clonality** (categorical: CLONAL/SUBCLONAL/INDETERMINATE) as "Plot Mutations by" axis options, gated on per-gene ASCN data availability.

## `PlotsTab.tsx`
- Add `MutationCountBy.CancerCellFraction` and `MutationCountBy.Clonality` enum values
- Gate dropdown options via `some()` across `cachedMutations`, checking `ccfExpectedCopies` / `clonal` per-field (not all-or-nothing)
- Reset `mutationCountBy` to a supported default when ASCN data is absent for the selected gene, preventing stale unsupported selection
- Update data-availability alert to accurately reflect that samples without mutations/clonality appear as "No mutation"/"Not profiled"

## `PlotsTabUtils.tsx`
- Use `_.isNumber` predicate instead of `_.compact()` when collecting CCF values — preserves valid `0`
- Filter `ClonalValue.NA` before `uniq()` in clonality axis data; return `null` if no valid values remain (excludes the sample, consistent with VAF/CCF behavior)
- Add label and category ordering for both new axis types

## Tests
- Unit tests in `PlotsTabUtils.spec.ts` covering CCF and Clonality axis-data generation

conditions per the WebSocket spec. The wrapper should just pass through

commit.message:
Add strand-aware pair filtering and ? junction report checks

find_split_genes.pl:
- Store strand from GFF into %locs (was only storing ref, start, end, coord)
- Reject cross-strand gene pairs in the main pair-building loop
  Two genes on opposite strands cannot be halves of the same split gene.
  Previously, a minus-strand gene could tile with a plus-strand gene via
  a shared reference protein region (e.g. ROBO case: two paralogs on
  opposite strands cross-paired via shared N-terminal domain coverage).
  The strand check prevents these false positive chains from forming.

merge_split_genes.pl:
- Add strand consistency check before building any merge
  If genes in a chain are on different strands, skip the merge and
  emit a WARNING to STDERR with merge_id and per-gene strand info.
  Acts as a safety net for chains from older finder output or
  alternative analysis paths.

split_gene_report_checks.sh:
- Update num_tiling_hits description to reflect corrected evolutionary
  reasoning (domain-boundary conservation, not gene family size;
  blind spot documented for genes with diverged paralogs)
- Update flag description block to explain ? in junction_tiling_hits
  and its two causes after the strand filter: different reference
  proteins (empty hit intersection) or heavy overlap on same reference
- Add query: chains with ? junctions (transitive joins, no direct tiling)
- Add query: chains where a terminal gene has ? evalue (no hit to chain's
  best reference protein — strongest indicator of misattribution)
- Add query: WEAK_END chains where terminal gene also has ? evalue
  (cross-check protein evidence against evalue absence)

fix(indexing): mark lazy-indexed repos as complete

Repos >= 200MB use the lazy indexing path which creates a LazyContentStore
for on-demand content fetching. This path never set indexingProgress.isComplete
to true, causing codebaseAnalysis to never compute — leaving the UI stuck on
'Analyzing codebase...' forever.

Set isComplete to true after metadata-only indexing finishes. Only affects
repos that trigger the lazy content loading path (>= 200MB).

fix: normalize plain GitHub and skills.sh URLs in skills:add (#6)

* fix: normalize plain GitHub and skills.sh URLs in skills:add

Previously, add-skill.mjs only normalized URLs containing /blob/ or
/raw/. Plain GitHub URLs (https://github.com/owner/repo) were passed
raw to the skills CLI, which expects owner/repo shorthand — causing
the command to fail silently or behave unexpectedly.

Changes:
- Expand URL detection guard to catch any https:// URL
- Update normalizeGitHubUrl to return { source, skill? } instead of
  a plain string, supporting skills.sh URLs with embedded skill names
- Auto-inject --skill when parsed from a skills.sh URL

This lets users paste URLs directly from the browser or skills.sh:
  npm run skills:add -- https://github.com/owner/repo --skill name
  npm run skills:add -- https://skills.sh/owner/repo/skill-name

* Remove unused variable in add-skill.mjs

Remove unused variable 'orig' in URL normalization logic.

* Bump version from 0.1.4 to 0.1.5

* fix: update normalizeGitHubUrl tests to match new structured return type
